headline: 3 Biggest Oil Giants: Buy, Sell or Hold?
summary: The three biggest U.S. oil majors are sending mixed signals at current prices: Chevron (NYSE:CVX) at $185.82 looks constructive, Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) at $146.60 looks constructive, and Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) at $55.47 warrants patience. A WTI spike to $114.58/bbl in April and a still-elevated $95.00/bbl print in early June have reset the math for every ... 3 Biggest Oil Giants: Buy, Sell or Hold?

headline: National Fuel Gas Rewards Shareholders With 4% Dividend Increase
summary: NFG lifts its dividend 4% after 124 years of payouts, backed by free cash flow, shale assets and regulated growth plans.

headline: Occidental Petroleum (OXY): Buy, Sell, or Hold Post Q1 Earnings?
summary: Occidental Petroleum’s 35% return over the past six months has outpaced the S&P 500 by 28.6%, and its stock price has climbed to $55.44 per share. This was partly due to its solid quarterly results, and the performance may have investors wondering how to approach the situation.

headline: 1 Cash-Producing Stock with Exciting Potential and 2 We Brush Off
summary: A company that generates cash isn’t automatically a winner. Some businesses stockpile cash but fail to reinvest wisely, limiting their ability to expand.
